summary: Session 1: docs/modules.yaml manifest reader (readModulesManifest, tolerant read, real YAML parse), module/moduleTitle threaded onto SessionSet + raw module key on SessionSetConfig (manifest-validated; unknown/absent/no-manifest degrade to the implicit module), and the fail-loud global set-name uniqueness check per the routed architecture ruling (repo-family + relPath identity from the existing worktree enumeration, one flagged winner row via duplicateNameError, readAllSessionSetsWithDiagnostics envelope, deduped re-arming console.error; the legitimate main+worktree merge is unchanged). 18 new unit tests; suite 1291 green; tsc/compile clean; no new lint problems. Rode along: fixed the pre-existing drift-guard CI failure (Set-088 doc quoted a banned bigram at verification-loop-remediation-2026-07.md:94; reworded). Verification: 2 rounds (gpt-5-4), no Critical/Major ever rated; all four actionable sub-findings fixed in flight (wrong-shape manifest warning + warning-precision gating + evidence-count reconciliation), one dismissed with citation (non-empty codeRoots would reject recommendation §2.4's own codeRoots: [] integration example), one was a workflow-order misread (verification runs pre-close by design). Loop stopped at the 2-round cap per the constitution's Minor-only/unrated rule; per-finding adjudication in s1-conventions.md. Playwright Layer 3 fails environmentally in this shell (Electron window never launches; reproduced identically at clean HEAD) - CI matrix is the effective Layer 3 gate.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s1-next-orchestrator-analysis.json, gemini-pro) recommends an Opus-class/high seat for Session 2's cross-surface Explorer module tier (host TS + webview client + ARIA/pixel-compat constraints + Playwright layer), where a visual regression's remediation cost exceeds the seat premium. Supervision points: the ModulePayload protocol contract, the ARIA attributes, and the Layer 3 assertions.
